task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The Orders sheet is active and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Orders' tab is selected at the bottom of the spreadsheet, and the sheet displays columns like Order ID, Product ID, and Price.

task_completion.description: A formula is entered in the Price column to match product IDs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The formula bar shows a formula (e.g., starting with '=VLOOKUP') when a cell in the Price column (C2:C6) is selected.

task_completion.description: The formula correctly references the Products sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The formula in the formula bar contains a reference to the 'Products' sheet (e.g., 'Products.A:B' or similar).

task_completion.description: The Price column in the Orders sheet displays the correct prices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cells in the Price column (C2:C6) show the values: 49.99, 29.99, 89.99, 15.99, and 12.50 respectively.

integrity.description: The agent used the spreadsheet GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet application interface is visible and interacted with, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent used a formula rather than manually typing the prices.
integrity.visual_evidence: When a cell in the Price column is selected, the formula bar shows a formula (like VLOOKUP) instead of a hardcoded number.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a genuine spreadsheet application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application has standard spreadsheet features like a ribbon/toolbar, formula bar, and sheet tabs, not a plain text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected prices in the Orders sheet (cells C2 to C6) are: 49.99, 29.99, 89.99, 15.99, 12.50. The formula used to populate these prices must contain 'VLOOKUP' and reference the 'Products' sheet.
